description_long: This object is a mature sunflower head, characterized by a large, circular disk densely packed with seeds at its center. Surrounding the disk are numerous elongated, yellow petals that are slightly wilted and curl outward, giving the flower a dramatic, sunburst appearance. The outer edge of the disk is encircled by a green, leafy involucre, which supports the petals and provides a contrasting color. The surface of the disk is textured and granular, indicating the presence of seeds. The petals are arranged in a somewhat irregular, radial pattern, with some overlapping and varying in height. No stem is visible, and the flower head appears to be detached from the rest of the plant.
description: A mature sunflower head with a dense, seed-filled disk and curling yellow petals surrounded by a green leafy base. The petals are slightly wilted and arranged in a dramatic, irregular radial pattern.
description_view_0: The sunflower head is seen from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the dense seed disk and the curling, yellow petals that radiate outward.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the right side of the flower, showing more pronounced curling and separation among the petals, with the green leafy base visible.
description_view_2: The flower head is viewed from the opposite side, revealing a more uniform arrangement of petals and a clear view of the green involucre.
description_view_3: This angle shows the left side of the sunflower, with petals appearing more irregular and some gaps between them, while the disk remains prominent.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a somewhat irregular radial pattern around the central disk, with some overlapping and curling outward. There is no strict symmetry, and the petals vary in length and orientation, typical of a mature sunflower.
